mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with low federal funds rates amplifies consumer spending power, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS yields negative or insignificant alpha in multivariate regression over 3-year rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with Rank IC below 0.02 and t-statistic less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns across rolling 3-year windows, with mean IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly change are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or predictive accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts coinciding with low unemployment rates signal synchronized strength in consumer confidence and labor markets, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high HOUST and low UNRATE yields negative forward returns or insignificant predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth confirms macroeconomic expansion, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when monetary policy remains accommodative.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during accommodative periods, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 and ICIR less than 0.5.
